description: Terminology glossary for consistent translations across all languages

terms.definition: Biblical Hebrew term meaning 'those who came from the sky'. Refers to the extraterrestrial civilization that created life on Earth.
terms.notes: Always capitalize. Do not translate as 'God' or 'gods'.

terms.definition: President of the Council of the Eternals on the Elohim planet. Led the scientific team that created life on Earth.
The new religious movement founded by Claude Vorilhon (Raël) based on messages from the Elohim.

translations.ja: ラエリアン・ムーブメント

terms.definition: Born Claude Vorilhon, the last prophet who received messages from the Elohim.
The current precessional age (1945-4105 CE) characterized by revelation and enlightenment.
The ~26,000 year precessional cycle, consisting of 12 ages of ~2,160 years each.
The slow wobble of Earth's axis that causes the equinox points to shift through the zodiac.
The hypothesis that extraterrestrial beings visited Earth in antiquity and influenced human civilizations.
The hypothesis that life was created by an intelligent agent rather than random processes.

translations.ja: インテリジェント・デザイン

terms.definition: The first book of the Bible, interpreted as an account of the Elohim's creation activities.
The name of this knowledge base, referencing Ezekiel's vision.
The governing body of the Elohim on their home planet, led by Yahweh. Makes decisions about the creation, custody, and ongoing relationship with humanity.
The canonical Jewish scriptures, comprising the Torah, Prophets, and Writings. Also known as the Tanakh.
The academic discipline of comparing myths across cultures to identify shared structures, motifs, and possible common origins.
The scientific field that designs and constructs new biological systems, including the de novo synthesis of organisms — the modern analogue of what the Elohim are described as having done on Earth.
The belt of constellations along the ecliptic through which the Sun, Moon, and planets appear to move. The precessional age framework uses the astronomical zodiac, not the astrological sign system.
terms.notes: Astronomical sense (the band of constellations) — distinct from astrological sign-set. In German prefer Tierkreis (astronomical) over Sternzeichen (astrological).

terms.definition: In the corpus's reading, the Hebrew *ha-satan* — 'the accuser' or 'the tester' — designates a faction in the divine council that argues against the confident plans of others. The corpus uses the Hebrew sense, not the later Christian-demonological one.
terms.notes: ru uses transliterated Hebrew '*ха-сатан*' (italicised) per 2026-06 project decision — preserves the philological argument the corpus is making about the Hebrew root, vs. conventional 'Сатана' which carries the Christian-demonological sense. zh/zh-Hant use Union Version (和合本) Protestant convention 撒但. he rendering: שָׂטָן (native form, with niqqud).

terms.definition: The first major section of 1 Enoch (chapters 1–36), recounting the descent of the Watchers — a class of divine beings — to Earth, their hybrid offspring (the Nephilim), and the divine response. A primary apocryphal source for the corpus's reading of pre-flood events.
terms.notes: The class of beings themselves: 'Watchers' / 'Veilleurs' / 'Стражи' / 'Wächter' etc. — the same translations apply with the 'Book of' prefix dropped. he: ספר הצופים (alternative: ספר העירים).

terms.definition: The Aboriginal Australian conception of the foundational creative period — neither past nor present in linear time but a continuously-active substratum of reality. Surfaces in the corpus as one of the cross-cultural traditions preserving the creation-narrative shape.
terms.notes: Often rendered as 'the Dreaming' with definite article. de 'Traumzeit' is the standard German anthropological term. ja uses katakana loanword; alternatives include ドリームタイム. he: הזמן החלום.

terms.definition: The Greek philosophical-tragic term for overweening pride that invites divine correction. The corpus uses it as a moral category recurring across traditions — most explicitly in the Crichton/Malcolm warning in the Preamble, and structurally in the Babel and Eden narratives.
terms.notes: Often italicised in body prose as a Greek loanword. fr keeps the Greek form 'hubris' (italicised) per French scholarly convention. de 'Hybris' is the naturalised German form (capitalised as all German nouns). zh/zh-Hant 傲慢 conveys the moral sense but loses the Greek-tragic connotation; consider keeping 'hubris' transliterated in Latin script alongside on first mention. ru 'хюбрис' is one of several competing transliterations (also: гюбрис); verify with native-speaker reviewer on first canonical use. he: הוּבְּריס (transliterated Greek with niqqud).

terms.definition: Giorgio de Santillana and Hertha von Dechend's 1969 study reconstructing precessional cosmology as a cross-cultural framework preserved in world mythology. One of the corpus's two interpretive backbones (the Raëlian source material being the other).
terms.notes: Book title — preserve in italics as a work title. zh-Hant variant '哈姆雷特之磨' is closer to literary Chinese register; '哈姆雷特的磨坊' is also acceptable. ja '臼' (mortar/mill) emphasises the cosmological grinding-stone metaphor. he: standard practice keeps the English title with a Hebrew transliteration in parens (טחנת המלט).

terms.definition: Ancient Greek poet (c. 700 BCE) whose *Theogony* and *Works and Days* preserve the five-ages scheme (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Heroic, Iron) that the corpus reads as a Greek variant of the cross-cultural age-of-the-world framework.
terms.notes: Standard scholarly transliterations across languages. Adjectival form ('Hesiodic') derives regularly in each language (de: hesiodisch; fr: hésiodique; ja: ヘシオドスの; etc.). he: הסיודוס.

terms.definition: The precessional age of Capricorn — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Sagittarius — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Scorpio — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Libra — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Virgo — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Leo — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Cancer — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Gemini — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Taurus — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Aries — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The precessional age of Pisces — one of the twelve approximately 2,160-year ages that structure the Wheel of Heaven corpus's main sequence. Astronomical (precessional), not astrological.
The patriarch who built the ark in Genesis 6–9 and survived the flood. In the corpus's reading, the historical figure preserved as the genetic-and-cultural seed of the post-flood Eden lineage.
terms.notes: he: נח (no niqqud in modern usage; נֹחַ with niqqud in liturgical contexts).

terms.definition: The vessel built by Noah to survive the flood. In the corpus's reading, a preservation craft prepared in advance of an engineered or anticipated cataclysm.
terms.notes: he: תיבת נח.

terms.definition: The structure described in Genesis 11 whose construction prompted the divine confusion of languages. In the corpus's reading, a post-flood human technological project that the Elohim intervened to disrupt.
terms.notes: he: מגדל בבל. German uses Luther's 'Turm zu Babel'.

terms.definition: The cities destroyed by divine fire in Genesis 19. In the corpus's reading, a targeted Elohim intervention against a specific human population.
terms.notes: he: סדום ועמורה. zh/zh-Hant use Union Version (和合本) Protestant convention.

terms.definition: The patriarch of Genesis 12–25, founder of the Eden lineage's covenantal phase. In the corpus's reading, the figure with whom the Council establishes the long-arc lineage that becomes the Hebrew people.
terms.notes: he: אברהם. Spanish alt 'Abrahán' is less common in modern usage.

terms.definition: The leader of the Exodus and lawgiver of Sinai in the Hebrew Bible. In the corpus's reading, the figure who receives the Sinai theophany — read as a high-bandwidth Elohim contact event.
terms.notes: he: משה. German prefers 'Mose' (Luther) over the Latin 'Moses'; both occur.

terms.definition: The departure of the Israelites from Egypt under Moses, narrated in the biblical book of the same name. In the corpus's reading, an Elohim-led extraction event with full theophanic apparatus.
terms.notes: he: ספר שמות (book) / יציאת מצרים (the event). The biblical-book form is used here; for the event qua narrative, use the event form contextually.

terms.definition: Jesus of Nazareth, central figure of the New Testament and founder of the Christian tradition. In the corpus's reading, the most consequential of the Piscean-age prophetic figures sent under the post-discovery missionary strategy.
terms.notes: he: ישוע (Yeshua, common scholarly form) or יֵשׁוּעַ with niqqud.

terms.definition: The first and best of the five ages in Hesiod's *Works and Days* (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Heroic, Iron). In the corpus's reading, the Hesiodic scheme is a Greek variant of the cross-cultural age-of-the-world framework that the precessional ages also preserve.
terms.notes: he: תור הזהב. Distinct from the corpus's precessional 'great year' concept (see [[great-year]]).

terms.definition: The conflict in the celestial realm described in Revelation 12 and analogous passages across traditions (Vedic devas vs. asuras, Norse Aesir-Vanir war, Mesopotamian Anunnaki conflicts). In the corpus's reading, a faction-conflict within the Elohim governance that is preserved across multiple cultural memories.
terms.notes: he: מלחמה בשמיים. fr alternative: 'Guerre dans le Ciel'.

terms.definition: A single day of the seven-day creation sequence in Genesis 1. In the corpus's reading, each 'day' is mapped to one precessional age (the 1st day → Capricorn, etc.), making the Genesis 1 narrative a compressed cosmological framework rather than a literal seven-24-hour-day account.
terms.notes: Ordinals constructed regularly per language (1st Day → 1. Schöpfungstag / 1er jour de la Création / 創造の第1日 / 창조의 첫째 날 / 1-й день Творения / 创造的第一日 / 創造的第一日 / היום הראשון לבריאה). he: יום הבריאה.

terms.definition: The hypothetical engineering of a planetary surface and atmosphere to make it habitable. The corpus reads several precessional ages (Sagittarius / Scorpio / Libra) as terraforming phases preceding the biological-creation phases.
terms.notes: Wiki slug `terraforming` resolves to a corpus entry.

terms.definition: The scientific discipline studying life beyond Earth — its possible forms, detection signatures, and the conditions under which it can arise. The corpus's 'scientific overlay' on the Capricorn-age survey-and-target-selection phase tracks contemporary astrobiology's instrumentation directly.

The planet of origin of the Elohim — the world from which they departed before arriving on Earth in the Capricorn age. The wiki entry treats the canonical-source claims about its star, distance, and conditions.

translations.es: planeta de origen de los Elohim

terms.notes: Short-form variants (Heimatplanet / 母星 / 故乡行星 / planète d'origine) are acceptable in body prose when the antecedent is clear; use the full form on first mention.

terms.definition: The original creation-site of the first humans in Genesis 2–3. In the corpus's reading, the laboratory or controlled environment in which the Elohim conducted the initial human-genesis work.
Hebrew Bible prophet (6th c. BCE) whose visions — especially the chariot of Ezekiel 1 — the corpus reads as direct first-person testimony of an Elohim craft seen at close range.
terms.notes: de uses Luther's 'Hesekiel'; Catholic German tradition sometimes uses 'Ezechiel'. zh uses Union Version (和合本) convention. ko uses 'Eseuk-gel' (에스겔, Protestant) — Catholic 한국 Bible uses 에제키엘.

terms.definition: Frank Drake's 1961 probabilistic formulation estimating the number of communicating civilisations in the Milky Way as the product of seven factors (star-formation rate, fraction with planets, fraction habitable, fraction with life, fraction intelligent, fraction communicating, average lifetime). The corpus uses the equation as the contemporary scientific frame for the Capricorn-age target-selection problem.

The James Webb Space Telescope (launched 2021, science operations from 2022) — the contemporary instrument whose transit-spectroscopy capability the corpus reads as the early-stage analogue of the long-range characterisation phase the Elohim are described as having conducted on Earth.

translations.ja: ジェイムズ・ウェッブ宇宙望遠鏡
translations.zh: 詹姆斯·韦伯太空望远镜
translations.zh-Hant: 詹姆斯·韋伯太空望遠鏡

terms.notes: Abbreviation 'JWST' is retained as-is across all languages (per international astronomy convention). Use the full name on first mention, then the abbreviation.

terms.definition: The nearest star to the Sun (~4.24 light-years), with a known terrestrial-mass planet in the habitable zone (Proxima b). The corpus uses it as the contemporary instrumentation benchmark for what's reachable with present-day technique.

translations.ja: プロキシマ・ケンタウリ

terms.notes: zh/zh-Hant use the calque '比邻星 / 比鄰星' (nearest-neighbour star) rather than transliteration; this is the standard PRC / Taiwan astronomy convention.

terms.definition: NASA's planned successor to Hubble/JWST (concept announced 2023; launch target ~2040s), designed to characterise atmospheric biosignatures of dozens of Earth-analogue worlds around nearby stars.

translations.ja: ハビタブルワールズ天文台

terms.notes: ru/de/he keep the English name (Russian astronomy-press convention; German scientific press often does the same). Translate the parenthetical descriptive gloss into the target language on first mention. Acronym HWO is universal.

terms.definition: The Breakthrough Initiatives interstellar-probe concept (announced 2016, design study ongoing) for laser-pushed gram-scale spacecraft to Alpha/Proxima Centauri on a ~20-year timeline. The corpus uses it as the contemporary candidate for the kind of long-range Capricorn-age survey approach.

translations.ja: ブレークスルー・スターショット

terms.notes: Program-name convention — most languages keep the English name. zh and zh-Hant render as 突破星击 / 突破星擊 (with 計畫 'program' in Taiwan tradition). ja and ko use katakana/hangul phonetic forms.

terms.definition: An exoplanet ~124 light-years away whose atmosphere shows tentative spectroscopic evidence of dimethyl sulphide (a possible biosignature), as observed by JWST in 2023–2025. The corpus uses it as the contemporary example of the kind of remote-detection result the Elohim survey would have generated.
terms.notes: Astronomical designation kept in Latin script across all languages per international astronomy convention. The same convention applies to all exoplanet / star catalogue designations (TRAPPIST-1, HD 209458 b, etc.).

terms.definition: An ancient Persian / Iranian unit of distance (roughly 5–6 km depending on era and surface). The corpus invokes it as the canonical ancient-measure analogue of the corpus's stated Elohim-home-planet distance figure.
terms.notes: Italicised in body as a foreign technical unit. he 'פרסה' is the cognate ancient unit (1 פרסה ≈ 4 mil ≈ 3.84 km in Talmudic sources); other langs use phonetic transliteration.

categories.core: Core terminology that defines the Raelian/Elohim narrative
categories.timeline: Terms related to cosmic ages and chronology
